add-new-agent-provider.blurb: Integrating a new coding-agent CLI into regin's capture pipeline
add-new-agent-provider.intent: Show a future agent the exact contract and integration points for teaching regin about a new coding-agent CLI (Kimi/Codex-style): subclass the AgentProvider adapter, register it, declare its capabilities/format flags, and wire path resolution, transcript parsing, hook install, permission shaping, and skill deployment so regin captures the agent's sessions without hard-coding vendor paths or payload shapes across the shared pipeline.
add-new-agent-provider.label: Adding a new agent provider (AgentProvider adapter playbook: contract, registry, hook/transcript wiring)

agent-memory.blurb: Capture / distil / store / recall / reflect / feedback / exemplars of cross-session memory — enter for tasks touching memory writes, recall, or lessons. Code: lib/memory/ (except evaluate.py)
agent-memory.intent: Capture / distil / store / recall / reflect / feedback / exemplars of cross-session memory — enter for tasks touching memory writes, recall, or lessons. Code: lib/memory/ (except evaluate.py)
agent-memory.label: Agent memory engine

agent-memory-architecture.intent: Orient a future agent in regin's cross-session memory subsystem before touching any of it: the thin module-verb facade in `__init__.py` (`remember`/`recall`/`reflect`/`get`/`update`/`forget`/`supersede`/`stats`), the four ports in `ports.py` (`EmbeddingProvider`, `LLMProvider`, `MemoryStore`, `MemorySink`) that every concrete provider is injected behind so no module under `lib/memory/` imports a concrete service, the edge adapters in `adapters.py` (`SkillRouterEmbedding` bi-encoder+cross-encoder, `ExternalAgentLLM`/`resolve_distiller`), and the `scoping.py` policy layer that stamps `global` / `repo:<name>` onto writes and recall. The single mutable `memories` table carries both tiers (`working`/`episodic`) via a `tier` column with its side tables (`memory_embeddings`, `memory_validations`, `injection_events`, `memory_edges`, `memory_topics`, `memory_authoritative_topics`, `memory_exemplars`/`topic_exemplars`, `topic_injections`/`topic_route_decisions`, `referent_session_df`); `models.py` declares its own `MetaData` so `regin init`/`rebuild` and Alembic never see memory tables, and `engine.py` is a self-initializing third engine (`db/regin_memory.db`) that runs `create_all` plus FTS5 DDL and idempotent rename/column migrations on first checkout.
agent-memory-architecture.label: Agent memory architecture (ports/adapters engine, self-initializing DB, tiers & lifecycle model)

aliases: supersede a progress message by key
where do send_to_user messages get stored
agent-messages-inbox.blurb: Changing how send_to_user messages are captured, stored, pushed, or shown in the Inbox.
agent-messages-inbox.intent: How a `send_to_user` MCP call becomes a durable, typed, supersedable message: the session-blind stdio server only declares the schema; the PostToolUse hook (which alone knows the session) persists it through the single `store.record_message` writer; high-severity types fan out to pluggable push channels; the web API + Vue Inbox render the per-session feed and the cross-session unread badge.
agent-messages-inbox.label: Agent → human message channel (send_to_user store, cross-session Inbox, push fan-out)

agent-providers.blurb: AgentProvider adapters (Claude / Kimi), transcript parsing, hook install — enter when integrating a new coding-agent CLI. Code: lib/providers/
agent-providers.intent: AgentProvider adapters (Claude / Kimi), transcript parsing, hook install — enter when integrating a new coding-agent CLI. Code: lib/providers/
agent-providers.label: Agent provider adapters

data-config.intent: ORM / SQLModel, db/schema.sql, settings, auth, multi-user, sync — enter when touching schema, settings, auth, or sync. Code: lib/orm/, lib/settings.py, lib/auth.py, lib/sync/
data-config.label: Data & config

eval-grading.blurb: LLM-judge session grading (rubric / correctness / coverage / grounding / agentic), recall-quality eval, pattern-conceal experiments, goal verification — enter for anything that measures quality or judges good/bad. Code: lib/grader/, lib/memory/evaluate.py, lib/experiments.py, lib/goal_feedback.py
eval-grading.intent: LLM-judge session grading (rubric / correctness / coverage / grounding / agentic), recall-quality eval, pattern-conceal experiments, goal verification — enter for anything that measures quality or judges good/bad. Code: lib/grader/, lib/memory/evaluate.py, lib/experiments.py, lib/goal_feedback.py
eval-grading.label: Evaluation & grading

aliases: pin the bar before the agent builds
hard-gate floor + opt-in lessons roadmap for a goal
reinforce included lessons, decay dropped ones
close the build loop back into agent memory
goal-verified-loop.blurb: Wiring `regin goal preflight`/`feedback`, the AreaRules routing table, or how a goal run pins gates and feeds memory.
goal-verified-loop.intent: The two halves of the `/goal-verified` loop-engineering workflow that live in regin (the build + verify halves are the skill). Preflight (`lib/goal_preflight.py`) is a small portable kernel: `build_roadmap` returns a `{goal, gates, lessons}` roadmap from a universal hard-gate floor (the test suite stays green + an independent fresh-context reviewer ran `/code-review high`) plus an opt-in, off-by-default flat-FTS lessons recall. The area-router (`AREA_RULES`/`detect_areas`/`resolve_references`) was retired 2026-06 because it matched goal prose by keyword and merely restated CLAUDE.local.md's file-keyed convention table; per-area machine gates and reference components now come from that table and from reading the real target files, and turning gates into falsifiable acceptance items is left to the consuming agent. Feedback (`lib/goal_feedback.py`) closes the loop after verification — included lessons are reinforced, FAILED acceptance items become new area-tagged `goal-verified-fail` lesson memories filed under agent-supplied authoritative topic nodes (exact id only, no fuzzy fallback) — reusing memory primitives only, adding no store of its own.
goal-verified-loop.label: Loop-engineering: goal preflight roadmap → verified build → feedback into memory

hooks-injection.blurb: hook_plugin, UserPromptSubmit / PostToolUse wiring, prompt-template injection — enter when tuning hook behaviour or injected content. Code: lib/hook_plugin.py, lib/prompt_templates.py
hooks-injection.intent: hook_plugin, UserPromptSubmit / PostToolUse wiring, prompt-template injection — enter when tuning hook behaviour or injected content. Code: lib/hook_plugin.py, lib/prompt_templates.py
hooks-injection.label: Hooks & injection

aliases: how past lessons reach the prompt
memory-auto-injection.intent: Show how recalled memories actually reach an agent and why the delivery path is shaped the way it is: the `UserPromptSubmit` handler (`hook_manager/handlers/memory_recall.py`) is a fresh short-lived process per prompt, so it borrows the warm `regin serve` embedder via a loopback-auth-exempt POST to `/api/memory/recall` (`_recall_via_server`) and cleanly falls back to in-process FTS-only (`_recall_fts`); speculative inject never reinforces (overlap-gated and same-session deduped via `injection_events`, recorded by `record_injections`) except for the earned `reinforce_resurfaced`; each inject (and the routed `<topic_context>` banner) is recorded so the feedback loops can later judge it; and the long-lived `recall` MCP server (`mcp_server.py`) is the deliberate deeper-pull path. Also covers the `memory.recall` span (`_emit_recall_span`) emitted into the trace so a reviewer can see exactly what was fed to each prompt.
memory-auto-injection.label: Memory auto-injection & on-demand recall (UserPromptSubmit hook, recall MCP server, /api/memory/recall)

aliases: the sleep cycle of agent memory
attach synthesis cluster to topic graph
memory-consolidation-reflect.intent: Explain `reflect()` — the batch consolidation cycle that turns raw `working` rows into curated `episodic` knowledge — step by ordered step: near-duplicate dedup (`_dedup_and_judge`, embedding cosine or text-ratio fallback), LLM-judged contradiction in the similarity gray zone, working→episodic promotion with `recall_count`-driven importance, a pre-decay engagement densifying sweep (`_score_pending` calling `feedback.rebuild_session_referent_df` + `score_pending_sessions`), importance decay for chronically-ignored rows whose gate reads the soft-vs-hard engagement bits (`_decay_chronically_ignored`/`_engagement_spare`), LLM synthesis of higher-order rules from cosine clusters (`_synthesize`), content-hash-skipped embedding (`_embed_episodic`), forget-stale retirement of never-recalled aged rows, and optional filesystem stale-ref flagging. Also covers the by-products: the undirected `related`-edge graph (`_harvest_edges`), emergent `memory_topics`, and `topic_attach.py` proposing merge/create onto the human-approved `.regin/topics` graph.
memory-consolidation-reflect.label: Memory consolidation (reflect: dedup, contradiction, promote, synthesize, forget-stale, decay, edges & topics)

memory-curation-surfaces.blurb: Curate the memory store by hand: approve/retire/forget memories, run reflect/distill, browse the taxonomy tree, tune exemplars & topic-route relevance, probe recall.
memory-curation-surfaces.intent: Point an agent at the human-facing controls for curating the cross-session memory store — the curation that is the entire point of memory being mutable. Covers the `/api/memory/*` Flask blueprint (`web/blueprints/memory.py`), the four-tab `MemoryView.vue` dashboard with its `frontend/src/components/memory/*` panels, and the `regin memory` Typer CLI mirror (`cli/commands/memory.py`). These are the three coordinated entry points for reviewing distill proposals, fixing a memory's veracity, walking the topic taxonomy, hand-curating signed exemplars, tuning topic-route relevance, and probing what a query would surface.
memory-curation-surfaces.label: Memory curation surfaces (Memory view, /api/memory/* blueprint, regin memory CLI)

aliases: implicit capture from a finished session
memory-distillation-capture.intent: Explain the two ways a memory is born and why distillation is LLM-only by contract: the explicit path (`send_to_user(type=lesson)` teed into the store by `post_tool_trace._remember_lesson` with span/agent/scope provenance, honoring a `supersedes` id), and the implicit agentic distiller (`distill.py::distill_session`) that reads a finished session's trace, is handed only the high-signal hints (`_grade_digest` grader findings + `_signal_digest` failure→fix / pushback chains) plus the read-only trace dump/span commands and self-fetches just the spans it needs so prompt size stays constant. Covers the self-scored importance band (`_finalize_status`: drop below `distill_min_importance`, auto-approve at/above `auto_approve_importance`, else queue as `proposed`), dedup-at-write reinforcement (`_dedup_candidate`/`_reinforce_existing`), and at-write contradiction supersede (`_supersede_candidate`, gated by `distill_supersede_on_conflict`) — the curation gate that keeps the store from filling with session-narrating noise.
memory-distillation-capture.label: Memory capture & distillation (send_to_user lesson tee + agentic post-session distiller)

aliases: did the injected memory actually help
referent appears in a later span
soft vs hard ignore decay signal
memory-engagement-feedback.intent: Explain the half of the recall loop that learns whether a speculatively-injected memory helped: `score_injection_usefulness` pulls concrete referents (file paths, backtick identifiers) out of each injected memory (`_referents`) and checks whether any appear in a tool span that fired *after* injection (`_post_injection_spans` — the ordering gate is load-bearing), abstaining when there are no referents. Covers the idf weighting that discounts corpus-ubiquitous referents (precomputed session document-frequency in `referent_session_df` via `rebuild_session_referent_df`, gated by `engagement_idf_min_weight`), the `engaged`/`matched` bits stamped once per `injection_events` row (`_stamp_event`) so the decay gate can tell a soft ignore from a hard one, the importance reward for engaged hits (`_reward_engaged`), the reflect-time `score_pending_sessions` densifying sweep, and how this positive signal both records query exemplars and pairs with reflect's negative decay half.
memory-engagement-feedback.label: Memory engagement feedback (inject→usefulness verdict, idf-weighted referents, decay gate coupling)

aliases: why is this topic route suppressed for some prompts
thumbs up down on a routed topic banner
memory-exemplar-rescore.intent: The signed query-exemplar primitive, now **topic-route only**. An exemplar is the embedding of a prompt a topic banner was injected on, tagged `polarity` +1 (engaged) / -1 (ignored) and `source` `auto` (feedback-captured) | `manual` (human-curated); `topic_exemplars` rows are kept per (topic, model, polarity), each polarity capped at `negative_max_per_memory`. At route time the max cosine of the incoming query to a topic's negatives/positives gates the `<topic_context>` banner — suppress when it resembles past hard-ignores (`topic_negative_suppress_sim`), protect when it resembles past engagements — query-locally, with nothing written to the topic row. Covers the auto write path (topic-route feedback → `add_topic_negatives` / `add_topic_positives` → `add_topic_exemplars`), the manual curate path (`POST /api/memory/exemplars` topic-only, `/api/memory/topic-feedback`, the `/api/memory/topic-route-preview` playground, `regin memory exemplar-add` / `exemplar-rm`), and per-row revert. **History:** the parallel memory-recall rescore (`memory_exemplars`, `store._apply_exemplar_rescore`, `negative_demotion_weight` / `positive_boost_weight`, `MemoryExemplars.vue`, the `GET /api/memory/exemplars` summary) was **removed** in 45bbe76 — its positive half was dead and the negative half showed no measured recall lift; recall now rides the dense + cross-encoder rerank stack plus importance decay, and lessons surface via the topic-tree walk (`memory-tree-nav`). The routing-feedback loop that consumes this primitive is `memory-topic-route-feedback`; design note `docs/agent-memory-exemplars.md`.
memory-exemplar-rescore.label: Signed query exemplars — topic-route suppression/protection (memory-recall rescore removed)

aliases: how a memory is scored and ranked
hybrid retrieval over the memory corpus
memory-recall-pipeline.intent: Explain the read-side ranking stack a `recall()` request flows through in `store.py`, so an agent debugging or tuning recall knows which knob moves which result: a `pattern_router`-shaped hybrid retrieve (`_lexical_ids` FTS5/BM25 leg + `_dense_ids` brute-force-cosine over `memory_embeddings` with `_lazy_backfill` + `_rrf` fusion + `_order_candidates` cross-encoder rerank), each stage degrading to the next without an embedder so an FTS-only path always works; the bounded `_quality_factor` ([0.9,1.3], gated by `recall_quality_weighting`) that re-ranks proven/recent memories by importance, veracity, `recall_count`, and recency without overriding relevance; `_apply_topic_boost` for memories linked to an authoritative topic node; optional `_mmr_select` diversity (`inject_mmr_lambda`) and opt-in 1-hop `_expand_via_edges` (`recall_expand_*`); the `expand.py` LLM query rewrite front-end for terse prompts; and `_bump_recall` reinforcement on deliberate recall. Measured by the FTS-only `evaluate.py` regression harness. The contextual exemplar rescore stage that also runs here has its own topic.
memory-recall-pipeline.label: Memory recall pipeline (FTS + dense + RRF + rerank, quality weighting, MMR, edge expansion, query expansion)

memory-topic-route-feedback.intent: Explain the relevance feedback loop wrapped around authoritative-topic routing — the topic analog of memory engagement feedback. The `UserPromptSubmit` hook records each injected `<topic_context>` banner as a `TopicInjection` row; the grader's `InjectedRelated` aspect (`config/settings.json` + `lib/grader/service._maybe_apply_injection_relevance`) judges whether the banner fit the user's goal and `store.apply_topic_relevance` stamps a `satisfied`|`needs_revision`|`fail` verdict once per session. Aggregated per topic (`topic_relevance_summary`), a recurring fail rate over `topic_relevance_min_scored` / `topic_relevance_fail_rate` only *proposes* suppression — withholding is human-in-the-loop: a `TopicRouteDecision` of `suppressed` makes the hook's `_route_topic` withhold the banner, `allowed` pins it on, and no row means `auto`. A `fail` also becomes a -1 topic exemplar and a `pass` a +1, so `store.topic_route_suppressed` can withhold query-locally when the incoming prompt's cosine to a topic's negatives clears `topic_negative_suppress_sim` unless a positive protects it. The route playground (`store.topic_query_signals` → `/api/memory/topic-route-preview`, `TopicRoutePlayground.vue` / `MemoryTopicFeedback.vue`) probes what a query would route to and why.
memory-topic-route-feedback.label: Topic-route relevance feedback (InjectedRelated verdict, fail-rate proposal, human suppression gate, route playground)

aliases: how other users see my topics
multi-user-topic-sharing.blurb: Drill in when an approved topic shows on one machine but not a teammate's, or when wiring git-based topic sync across clones.
multi-user-topic-sharing.intent: Explain how regin's standalone-per-user model lets one user's approved topics become visible to other users: what travels via git (topic.json + wiki/<id>.md), what stays local (topic.local.json overlay, snapshot DB, in-flight proposals), and the producer/consumer steps (promote -> pre-commit add -> teammate git pull -> post-merge `topics import` -> snapshot DB seeded -> routable & visible in WebUI). Also covers the shared-server alternative when cross-user proposal review is needed.
multi-user-topic-sharing.label: Multi-user topic sharing (how approved topics travel from one user to another)

ops-cli.blurb: cli commands, doctor, audit, activity_log, db_rebuild, token accounting, search — enter when running the CLI, diagnosing the environment, or inspecting the activity log. Code: cli/, lib/doctor.py, lib/tokens/
ops-cli.intent: cli commands, doctor, audit, activity_log, db_rebuild, token accounting, search — enter when running the CLI, diagnosing the environment, or inspecting the activity log. Code: cli/, lib/doctor.py, lib/tokens/
ops-cli.label: CLI & ops

pattern-experiments.intent: Understand the experiment system that conceals selected H2/H3 sections of a pattern's SKILL.md at deploy time so rule-trigger rates can be compared against an unconcealed baseline. Covers the pure conceal filter, the experiments table and RuleTrigger.experiment_id linkage, the one-active-per-pattern lifecycle, the redeploy hook into skill_sync/skill_deployer, the baseline-vs-variant rollup endpoints, the Vue UI surfaces, and the gap between the ingest path and the rollup's variant column.
pattern-experiments.label: Pattern Conceal Experiments

patterns-skills.blurb: Local pattern guides + skill deploy/read — enter when writing a pattern, deploying a skill, or editing the skill index. Code: lib/patterns/, lib/skills/
patterns-skills.intent: Local pattern guides + skill deploy/read — enter when writing a pattern, deploying a skill, or editing the skill index. Code: lib/patterns/, lib/skills/
patterns-skills.label: Patterns & skills

proposal-review-comments.intent: Document the inline review-comment system layered on top of a proposal run: how a thread is anchored (general / topic field / proposal summary / wiki range), what HTTP endpoints CRUD threads and comments, how the ORM stores them with revision pointers, how `format_review_feedback_for_prompt` injects open threads into the next regenerate, and how the auto-`addressed` sweep closes threads whose anchored content actually changed.
proposal-review-comments.label: Proposal review comments (feedback threads anchored to topics & wiki)

recall-eval-verification.intent: Proving a recall change actually works: verify end-to-end through the real auto-inject path (not units), why silently-degrading fast paths and ungated FTS fallbacks hide breakage, embed-coverage completeness, mechanical probes that must not reinforce, and --json output hygiene. Drill in before trusting a recall eval.
recall-eval-verification.label: Recall eval & end-to-end verification (true inject path, silent-degradation gotchas)

aliases: where regin logs its own operations
tail and grep the activity log
regin-activity-log-observability.blurb: Inspecting or extending regin's own ops history — activity log, structured logging, or the dashboard audit trail
regin-activity-log-observability.intent: Map regin's self-observability stack — distinct from `lib/trace/` session traces. `lib/activity_log.py` writes one rotating `feature`-tagged JSONL stream (read=DEBUG / write=INFO discipline, secret-key auto-redaction), surfaced from the terminal via the `regin logs` group (list / tail / grep / prune / path). `lib/logging_setup.py` bootstraps structlog so Flask/SQLAlchemy/lib all emit into one pipeline with console vs. JSON rendering modes. `lib/audit.py` records who changed what in the web dashboard (best-effort, never blocks, routed through AuthSessionLocal). Use this when diagnosing regin's behaviour, not an agent session.
regin-activity-log-observability.label: Ops observability: activity log, structured logging & audit trail (regin's own operations history vs. session traces)

aliases: how the regin command line is wired
where do CLI command bodies live
regin-cli-architecture.blurb: Adding, grouping, or debugging a `regin` CLI command, or running doctor / init / rebuild
regin-cli-architecture.intent: Explain how the `regin` CLI is assembled and how to extend it: the thin `cli/regin.py` entrypoint that re-execs under the project `.venv` and fixes sys.path, the Typer root in `cli/app.py` that wires per-domain modules under `cli/commands/` (each exposing `register(app)` for flat commands or a pre-built sub-Typer for `regin <group> <cmd>`), the root callback that stamps every invocation into the activity log, and the shared `cli/deps.py` (`require_db` guard) + `cli/output.py` (testable echo/error/table sinks) helpers. doctor and db init/rebuild are the canonical ops commands.
regin-cli-architecture.label: regin CLI architecture & command surface (Typer entrypoint, venv re-exec, per-domain command registry, doctor/db lifecycle, add-a-command playbook)

rule-engine-design.intent: Explain how regin loads, dispatches, and executes lint/rewrite rules: the engine-agnostic Protocol, the three-source registry precedence (explicit settings → legacy grit fallback → autodiscovered bundles), the three concrete adapters (Grit, Bundle, Radon), the PostToolUse hook that drives them per edit, the two in-tree example bundles under example/rule/, and the step-by-step playbook for adding a new engine. Use this when adding a new engine kind, packaging a new rule pack as a bundle, wiring an existing engine into a new language, importing one of the example bundles, or debugging why a rule fired (or didn't) on a file.
rule-engine-design.label: Rule engine design (adapter protocol, registry, PostToolUse driver, in-tree example bundles, add-an-engine playbook)

rule-engines.blurb: GritQL / Radon / Bundle lint engines, language adapters, PostToolUse enforcement — enter when changing a lint rule or engine. Code: lib/rule_engines/, lib/rules/, lib/languages/
rule-engines.intent: GritQL / Radon / Bundle lint engines, language adapters, PostToolUse enforcement — enter when changing a lint rule or engine. Code: lib/rule_engines/, lib/rules/, lib/languages/
rule-engines.label: Rule engines (lint/rewrite)

aliases: get my session id from a bash command
why bash can't see the session id
session-id-probe.blurb: Debugging or extending how a Bash command recovers its own Claude Code session id.
session-id-probe.intent: Claude Code never hands the live session id to Bash, but every hook payload carries it. The PreToolUse hook `record()`s a `{sid, ts, nonce}` stamp into a small atomic JSON cache on each Bash call; the always-present `regin session-id` subcommand `resolve()`s the freshest entry by cwd (or an explicit `--nonce`). This durable cache replaces the old command-rewriting probe so `SID=$(… session-id)` works through `$(...)` and the full interpreter form alike — relied on by `goal preflight --session-id`, `goal feedback --trace-id`, and the `gate` anti-skip checks.
session-id-probe.label: Session-id probe (recovering the live Claude Code session id inside a Bash command)

session-trace.blurb: OTel-style span ingest, aggregates, merge, trace UI data — enter when changing trace capture, span attribution, or aggregation. Code: lib/trace/
session-trace.intent: OTel-style span ingest, aggregates, merge, trace UI data — enter when changing trace capture, span attribution, or aggregation. Code: lib/trace/
session-trace.label: Session trace

session-trace-design.intent: Explain how Claude Code sessions are captured as OpenTelemetry-style spans by hook handlers, persisted through the trace_service ingest layer into session_spans plus a denormalized sessions row, and rendered by the Vue SessionTraceView via the /api/sessions/* read endpoints.
session-trace-design.label: Session trace design (OTel-style spans, ingest pipeline, aggregates, dashboard read paths)

topic-graph.blurb: The topic taxonomy itself: routing, proposals, review, multi-user sharing, wiki — enter when changing topic structure, routing, or proposals. Code: lib/topics/
topic-graph.intent: The topic taxonomy itself: routing, proposals, review, multi-user sharing, wiki — enter when changing topic structure, routing, or proposals. Code: lib/topics/
topic-graph.label: Topic graph & routing

topic-proposal-pipeline.intent: Explain the end-to-end funnel that turns a user's topic request into one row in `.regin/topics/topic.json`: an external tool-using agent drafts the JSON+wiki, regin validates and persists it via the ORM, reviewers iterate through revise/regenerate/restore/stop, and a single server-side /diff+/apply path (or the matching library accept/replace/merge helpers) promotes a topic into the approved graph. Also describes the cross-thread cancel machinery that lets a Stop request terminate the agent subprocess without racing the worker's terminal-state writes.
topic-proposal-pipeline.label: Topic proposal pipeline (request → draft → review → apply/stop)

aliases: resolve a query into topic context
topic-routing.blurb: How a query becomes approved topic context: the matcher, keyword scoring, the route envelope, the --wiki output, and the topic-router skill.
topic-routing.intent: Understand how a freeform query resolves to approved topic context. Covers the priority-ordered matcher in lib/topics/route.py (alias/label exact -> ref-path exact -> ref-path substring -> label/intent substring, then a fuzzy multi-keyword fallback), the keyword-signal model (a wordfreq English-rarity prior in route.py scaled by the repo-adaptive query-log document-frequency multiplier in lib/topics/term_weights.py, plus a per-graph saturation filter that drops corpus-common terms), the route_topic envelope that returns ordered refs + bounded wiki pages + first-degree related topics with an approved/unmatched status, the best_topic_for_text reverse signal for long text, the generated topic-router skill, and the regin topics route/route --wiki/router-skill/rebuild-query-df CLI commands.
topic-routing.label: Topic routing (keyword router over the approved graph + topic-router skill)

trace-merge-reconcile.intent: The READ-time assembly path in merge.py: serve-time merge_spans, lookahead reattachment of UserPromptSubmit spans, dropped/discarded vs deleted render states, count-vs-items divergence, cancelled-tool span loss, segment-aware ctx%, and prompt-edit forks vs real /rewinds. Drill in when spans exist but render wrong.
trace-merge-reconcile.label: Trace serve-time merge & reconcile (reparent, reattach, forks/rewinds, render states)

trace-span-capture.intent: Adding or reshaping a span TYPE: a new _TOOL_BUILDERS entry, its payload schema, the attr-whitelist for an injected block, wiring a new traced system-event subtype across the capture layers, or normalizing a non-Claude provider's transcript (Kimi) into spans. Drill in when the question is 'how do I make X show up as a span'.
trace-span-capture.label: Trace span capture (builders, payload schemas, new system subtypes, provider quirks)

trace-usage-billing.intent: Token and dollar accounting for a session: turn_usage rows, why a Task-subagent's API spend goes missing, Workflow billing $0 from a cache-less rate shard, subagent transcript nesting depth, and gating the seen-cache on ingest success. Drill in when the bill or token rollup looks wrong.
trace-usage-billing.label: Trace usage & billing (turn_usage, token/cost accounting, subagent bill attribution)

unclassified.blurb: Topics awaiting a human bucket assignment. Appearing here is a curation backlog item to place under a real bucket.
unclassified.intent: Topics awaiting a human bucket assignment. Appearing here is a curation backlog item to place under a real bucket.
unclassified.label: Unclassified

webui.blurb: All Vue 3 SPA views + Flask /api blueprints — enter when changing a frontend page or its API. Code: frontend/, web/
webui.intent: All Vue 3 SPA views + Flask /api blueprints — enter when changing a frontend page or its API. Code: frontend/, web/
webui.label: Web UI & API

webui-dev-loop.intent: Environment/iteration mechanics around working on the UI, not the UI itself: restart the vite dev server after vite.config proxy changes, tmux panes not inheriting the spawner's PATH (absolutise argv[0]), and send_to_user links being file paths not URLs. Drill in when the dev loop misbehaves rather than the rendered surface.
webui-dev-loop.label: WebUI dev-loop mechanics (vite proxy restart, tmux PATH, send_to_user link form)

webui-styling.intent: CSS/render-level fixes in the Vue SPA: scoped styles not crossing into extracted child components (use global classes), data-theme palette inversion, Reka/Radix portalled content escaping scoped styles, the .input base class vs Tailwind utilities, focus-ring-as-containment, the Icon primitive (no emoji/inline svg), and measuring table-fixed cell overflow by text Range not the td box.
webui-styling.label: WebUI styling & layout gotchas (scoped CSS reach, theming, portalled content, table overflow)

aliases: what screens does the dashboard have
where is the view for this route
webui-surface.intent: Give a future agent a one-stop index of every page in the regin dashboard: which Vue view renders each route, how the routes are grouped in the sidebar, which feature flags hide a group, and how the SPA is built and served by Flask. It is a navigational map that hands off to the deep feature topics (memory, trace, rules, topics) rather than re-explaining them.
webui-surface.label: WebUI surface map (Vue 3 SPA pages, router routes, sidebar nav groups & the Flask shell that serves them)
